message:
test(fcp-crypto): add byte-exact COSE_Sign1 golden harness + unimplemented-variant marker

Add crates/fcp-crypto/tests/cose_golden.rs (352 lines) — a byte-exact
golden harness for the COSE_Sign1 encoding path exposed via
CoseToken::sign / to_cbor / from_cbor. The crate already had inline
round-trip tests, but no on-disk goldens pinning the exact wire bytes.
Any drift in RFC 9052 encoding (protected-header CBOR canonicalization,
claims map ordering, Ed25519 signature layout) would slip past those
round-trips because encode→decode symmetry is preserved even under
most encoder-side drift.

## Determinism contract pinned

COSE_Sign1 encoding MUST be deterministic for fixed inputs. The
harness leans on three already-deterministic layers:

  1. Ed25519 signatures are deterministic by construction (RFC 8032).
  2. CwtClaims::to_cbor maps to ciborium canonical encoding for
     integer-keyed maps (stable key-sort + shortest-form length
     encoding).
  3. Protected headers are frozen (alg = EdDSA, kid = 32-byte KID).

Key material is seed-derived (`[0x01; 32]`, `[0x02; 32]`, ...) so
the goldens can be reproduced by anyone with cargo + the fcp-crypto
source. No /dev/urandom, no Utc::now(), no feature-flag variance.

## golden_path helper

Resolves tests/golden/cose/<name>.hex relative to CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR.
Kept out-of-line so each test stays a one-liner: sign, encode, hex-
encode, assert_golden_hex("name", &hex).

## assert_golden_hex helper

Three behaviors in one function:

  - Always emits `[cose-golden] name=... hex=...` to stderr so CI
    logs can reconstruct the full suite even when the on-disk goldens
    are missing (fresh checkout, sandboxed worker, etc.).
  - If the golden is missing OR UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 is set, writes the
    actual hex into the slot and returns cleanly. First-run auto-seed
    logs `[GOLDEN] seeded <path> (first run — commit the file to
    lock bytes)`; explicit recapture logs `[GOLDEN] updated <path>
    (UPDATE_GOLDENS=1)`.
  - Otherwise compares actual vs expected and writes a sibling
    .actual file on mismatch so the operator can diff locally before
    deciding whether to regenerate.

Re-capture command (same shape as other insta/golden workflows in
the workspace):

    UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test -p fcp-crypto --test cose_golden
    git diff crates/fcp-crypto/tests/golden/cose/

## 7 tests

  - cose_sign1_empty_claims_golden — CWT with no claims; baseline
    on the empty-map canonical encoding.
  - cose_sign1_single_op_capability_golden — one capability in the
    capability_token claim; pins the embedded CBOR map shape.
  - cose_sign1_multi_op_capability_golden — multiple operations;
    pins list-order stability inside the capability claim.
  - cose_sign1_multiple_kids_golden — swept across distinct KID
    values; pins that protected-header CBOR reorders correctly
    when the kid bytes change.
  - cose_sign1_decode_truncated_errors — negative: decode of a
    truncated COSE_Sign1 buffer MUST return a typed error, not panic.
  - cose_sign1_verify_wrong_key_errors — negative: verify with the
    wrong Ed25519 key MUST return a typed error, not silently accept.
  - cose_encrypt_and_cose_mac_are_unimplemented_marker — absence
    marker that asserts fcp-crypto does NOT implement COSE_Encrypt or
    COSE_Mac. Confidentiality is handled via HpkeSealedBox, integrity
    via Blake3Mac — neither is wrapped in COSE framing. The marker
    test exists so a future agent adding COSE_Encrypt support lands
    with golden coverage from day one rather than silently.

Note: this commit lands the harness + negative tests only. Goldens
under tests/golden/cose/*.hex will auto-seed on first run
(assert_golden_hex writes them); the seeded files get committed in a
follow-up once a human has reviewed the first-run hex values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
